What you call

Name the octave, ignore the note.

Name the pitch, any octave. Uses the chromatic pad.

Both at once. Two-stage catch.

Run type

No score pressure; leans into notes you miss.

One hundred drops, scored. The run you share.

Endless climb until you tap out.

The sound
FX · the dirt

A roaring bed under the tone.

Tones sometimes fade in, no clear hit.

freq50%

Shorter window before the drop closes.

span1.5 s

Pitch slips off true; round to nearest.

offset±35 ¢

A second tone crowds the true one.

apart12 st
level40%
the true tone may be lost

Tones clipped to varied lengths.

max1.6 s

A flood of sound after the tone.

flood35%

Tone roams left and right. Use both ears.

roam100%

The tone repeats on a timer.

echooff

A vibrato shudder around the center.

depth1.0×

Volume swells and fades within a range.

range–%
law

Access

DROP is built to be played by feel and sight, not just sound. Every drop is shown as color + motion + a letter, pulsed as haptics, and named in captions. Tune it to you.

HapticsVibrate on beats, hits, and misses (phone only)
CaptionsShow the note name & result as text
Letter tokens on pucksShow note letters, not color alone
High contrastPure-black field, brighter neons, thicker edges
Reduce motionCalm the pulses and flashes
Beat metronomeAudible click on each beat
Visual beat guidePulse the frame on every beat
Untimed modePucks wait at the line — catch with no time pressure
